studies.duration_additional_info: The program had an average duration of 10 weeks; the primary outcome was assessed from baseline to program-end.
studies.intervention_and_control_group: Single-arm study: 326 participants started the program; 264 (81.0%) completed the intervention. No control group.
studies.additional_info_about_trial_arms: Single arm: all participants received a remote digital care program (DCP) combining education and pelvic floor muscle training (PFMT) with real-time biofeedback, managed asynchronously by a physical therapist.
studies.collected_data: Primary outcome: Urinary Impact Questionnaire-short form (UIQ-7) change from baseline to program-end, analyzed via latent growth curve analysis (LGCA). Secondary outcomes: Pelvic Floor Impact Questionnaire (PFIQ-7) for impact of pelvic conditions; Patient Health Questionnaire-9 (PHQ-9) for depression; Generalized Anxiety Disorder-7 (GAD-7) for anxiety; Work Productivity and Activity Impairment (WPAI) questionnaire; intention to seek additional healthcare; engagement metrics; and participant satisfaction.
studies.key_outcomes_findings: Of 326 participants who started the program, 264 (81.0%) completed the intervention. Significant improvement on UIQ-7 was observed (mean change 8.8, 95% CI 4.7–12.9, p < 0.001), corresponding to a response rate of 57.3%. Improvements were also observed in all secondary outcomes. High satisfaction was reported (mean 8.9/10, SD 1.8). The study concluded that the remote DCP with biofeedback is feasible and safe for reducing UI-related symptoms in a real-world setting.

audit.study_title: Digital Care Program for Urinary Incontinence in Females: A Large-Scale, Prospective, Cohort Study.
